title: PSNY Stock 1-For-30 Reverse Split Nasdaq Compliance Announcement
text: Polestar announced 1-for-30 reverse stock split of American Depositary Shares to avoid Nasdaq delisting. Reverse split became effective December 9, 2025. Polestar notified by Nasdaq in October for not meeting exchange's listing rules regarding minimum bid price of $1. October notice indicated Polestar below US$1.00 minimum with 180 days to regain compliance. Companies use reverse splits to boost share price above $1 minimum required by major exchanges, allowing compliance regainment after deficiency notice and preventing delisting. Reverse split consolidates shares mechanically lifting price above $1. Split will not change value of investors' overall holdings. Ratio changed from 1 ADS per ordinary share to 1 ADS for every 30 ordinary shares. While addressing listing requirement, reverse split does not resolve underlying issues of large net losses and auditor's going concern warning. Polestar faced substantial headwinds including net losses of $1.558 billion in nine months of 2025, tariff uncertainty, and ongoing funding concerns.
